page: Microsoft Azure D96ps_v6 is a general-purpose server instance from the Dpsv6 family, powered by an ARM64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. With a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, hyperthreading is absent. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate highly efficient multi-core execution and memory subsystem capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks, including software compilation with Clang and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, reaching top-tier status in both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations. Single-core performance, such as single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores, falls into the average to strong categories.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture delivers high multi-threaded throughput and memory performance without the premium cost of specialized compute or memory-optimized hardware classes. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is highly suited for scale-out multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, database operations, Redis deployments, and medium-scale LLM inference text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ps_v6 is an ARM64 general-purpose server instance powered by a 96-core Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, translating to a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, with no hyperthreading or bundled local storage. The instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, as evidenced by outstanding stress-ng and Redis benchmark results. Single-core performance remains moderate. This hardware profile offers a cost-effective, high-density solution for multi-threaded workloads, making it highly suitable for software compilation, static web serving, database management, and Redis deployments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ps_v6 is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 96 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 384 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ps_v6 is an ARM64 server with 96 dedicated cores and 384 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 platform delivering exceptional multi-core throughput and memory bandwidth for balanced, scale-out enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz with no hyperthreading.
384.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing.
Optimized for database operations, Redis deployments, and static web serving.
No bundled local storage, requiring network-attached Premium Storage.
